summary: Iter 128 — Standard Compliance Settings module (GLOBAL + FIRM overrides). Backend pytest 17/17 PASSED (1 optional smoke skipped). All 4 endpoints work correctly: GET/PUT global, GET/PUT firm/{cid}. Persistence verified via Create→Read pattern. Validation working (400 on negative number, invalid rounding mode, empty payload). Full firm-override lifecycle passes: clear → save override → has_override=true & effective merges → clear → has_override=false. Role gating verified: company_admin PUT correctly returns 403 for BOTH global and firm endpoints; sub_admin is allowed by design (server.py require_role inheritance, line 1952 — same as every other admin endpoint in this codebase). FRONTEND: /compliance-settings screen loads correctly for super_admin, all fields render with current values, edit+save+reload persistence verified (edited ESIC threshold 21000→21500, reloaded, restored back). One CRITICAL frontend bug found: the firm-picker chips DO NOT render because compliance-settings.tsx:62 calls GET /admin/companies which returns 404 (correct route is /companies). This makes the entire firm-level override UI unreachable — 'Kankani Enterprises' chip never appears. Backend firm endpoints themselves work fine. One MINOR UX bug: success banner is cleared immediately after save because load() sets banner=null at line 71 (user never sees the green 'Standard settings saved' confirmation because it's clobbered by the reload). Global settings restored to defaults; Kankani firm override cleared; injected test sessions deleted. No real payroll data touched.

ui_bugs.issue: Firm-picker chips never render: compliance-settings.tsx:62 calls api('/admin/companies') which returns 404. The correct endpoint is '/companies' (server.py:6610). This breaks the entire firm-level override UI — users cannot select Kankani or any other firm to set overrides. Catch block swallows the error so the screen silently loses the feature.

Success banner cleared immediately after save. save() sets banner='ok' then calls load(); load() at line 71 does setBanner(null), wiping out the 'Standard settings saved' confirmation. User sees no success feedback. Fix: skip setBanner(null) in load() when called from save(), or set banner AFTER load().

integration_issues.issue: Cannot be exercised at all from the UI because the firm-picker never renders (see /admin/companies 404 bug). Backend endpoints themselves are working — verified end-to-end via pytest.

action_items: Fix compliance-settings.tsx:62 — change api('/admin/companies') → api('/companies') so firm chips actually render.

Fix compliance-settings.tsx:71 — do not clear the banner inside load() when it's invoked from save()/clearOverride(); pass a preserveBanner flag or set banner AFTER load(). Currently users never see the green success confirmation.

(Optional / policy question for the user) The endpoint require_role(["super_admin"]) is being satisfied by sub_admin because server.py:1952 grants sub_admin blanket inheritance of super_admin's reach. That is documented and system-wide, but if the intent for Compliance Settings is TRULY super_admin only (payroll-affecting numbers), switch to require_super_admin_strict() on the two PUT handlers.
critical_code_review_comments: compliance_settings.py:78, 148 — PUT handlers correctly use require_role(['super_admin']). However due to require_role's built-in sub_admin inheritance (server.py:1952), sub_admins can also save these globally-impacting statutory numbers. If that's not desired, use require_super_admin_strict() instead.
compliance_settings.py:80-87 — Firm 'clear' uses $unset + $setOnInsert upsert. Works correctly; verified GET returns has_override=false and empty overrides after clear.

compliance_settings.py:100 — In firm PUT: numeric branch uses `if k in payload and payload[k] is not None`, but rounding branch uses `if k in payload and payload[k] in _ROUND_MODES` — silently DROPS invalid rounding modes here (unlike the global PUT which returns 400). Inconsistent with the global handler — either both should 400 or both should silently drop.

compliance_salary.py:269 — statutory_cfg merge treats rounding keys as str via `str(v)` but does not validate they belong to _ROUND_MODES. Trust boundary OK because settings endpoint validates, but any direct caller could inject junk. Non-blocking.
compliance-settings.tsx:62 — Wrong API route ('/admin/companies' → 404). The catch swallows the error so users just see an empty firm-picker.
compliance-settings.tsx:71 — setBanner(null) in load() destroys success/error banners set by save(). UX regression.

compliance-settings.tsx:110 — Save payload includes pf_rounding & esic_rounding even when scope='standard' AND user only changed one field. This means the current standard rounding values are always re-written — harmless but touches updated_at on every save.

success_rate.backend: 17/17 = 100% (1 optional smoke skipped: no compliance-salary preview endpoint discovered under the two candidate paths tried)
success_rate.frontend: 3/6 flows verified via UI (page load, field render, standard save+reload persistence). 3/6 flows blocked (firm-picker chip render, firm override save, firm override clear) — all blocked by same /admin/companies 404 bug. Verified via backend pytest instead.

seed_data_creation: Ephemeral only: (a) injected a user_sessions doc for user_0a38839e3568 (Kankani company_admin) to test role gating — deleted after run; (b) briefly wrote firm overrides on cmp_527fecdd7c during lifecycle test — cleared inside the test; (c) briefly wrote global settings (pf_percent_employee=11, esic_gross_threshold=21500, pf_rounding=floor) — restored to original values by the autouse restore_global fixture. Post-run verification: global settings == defaults, Kankani has_override == false.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Kankani company_admin has no working password (memo says 'PASSWORD & PIN CHANGED BY USER (unknown)') and imported PINs are 4-digit while backend requires 6-digit. To test company_admin auth, inject a session doc into user_sessions for user_0a38839e3568 (auth_method='test_injected' recommended so cleanup is a simple delete_many). urllib POSTs from the container get Cloudflare-403'd — use page.evaluate() with browser fetch for login, or curl (which works). Frontend firm-picker cannot be tested until /admin/companies → /companies is fixed. Backend routes for compliance settings are under /api/admin/compliance-settings and /api/admin/compliance-settings/firm/{company_id}.
rca_of_issue: Frontend Bug #1 (firm-picker missing chips): compliance-settings.tsx line 62 calls api('/admin/companies'). Backend does not define GET /api/admin/companies (only /api/companies at server.py:6610 and /api/admin/companies/{company_id}/* subpaths). Curl confirms 404. Fix: change '/admin/companies' → '/companies' in compliance-settings.tsx:62. Frontend Bug #2 (banner disappears): compliance-settings.tsx save() sets banner ok then awaits load(); load() unconditionally does setBanner(null) at line 71 destroying the success message. Fix: either skip setBanner(null) in load() when preserveBanner is true, or move the setBanner('ok', ...) call to AFTER load() completes.
